provenance.source_corpus: A Carex species-dominated marsh community represents the best short-term target for reclaiming wet meadow habitat following oil sands mining in Alberta, Canada

source_spans.text: Oil sands mining creates vast areas of disturbed land with contaminated sediment and sub-saline water that require reclamation.
Oil sands mining creates vast areas of disturbed land with contaminated sediment and sub-saline water that require reclamation.

nodes.name: Exposure to oil sands process-affected water

source_spans.text: exposure to oil sands process-affected water does not determine the vegetation community that will develop in reclaimed oil sands wetlands.
exposure to oil sands process-affected water does not determine the vegetation community that will develop in reclaimed oil sands wetlands.
The majority of vegetation communities that develop in reclaimed wetlands produce less aboveground biomass than natural wetlands
The majority of vegetation communities that develop in reclaimed wetlands produce less aboveground biomass than natural wetlands
In general, oil sands reclaimed wetlands have lower levels of sediment nutrients and lower sediment water content than natural wetlands
In general, oil sands reclaimed wetlands have lower levels of sediment nutrients and lower sediment water content than natural wetlands
In general, oil sands reclaimed wetlands have lower levels of sediment nutrients and lower sediment water content than natural wetlands

In general, oil sands reclaimed wetlands have lower levels of sediment nutrients and lower sediment water content than natural wetlands, and these deficiencies may be limiting vegetation community biomass production compared to natural wetlands.

In general, oil sands reclaimed wetlands have lower levels of sediment nutrients and lower sediment water content than natural wetlands, and these deficiencies may be limiting vegetation community biomass production compared to natural wetlands.
The closer reclaimed wetlands are to propagule sources, the more likely viable vegetation propagules will arrive on reclaimed substrates from nearby sources.
The closer reclaimed wetlands are to propagule sources, the more likely viable vegetation propagules will arrive on reclaimed substrates from nearby sources.

nodes.name: Fertilization with N, P and K

source_spans.text: Fertilization with N, P and K increased production when water was not a limiting factor
Fertilization with N, P and K increased production when water was not a limiting factor

nodes.name: Fertilization of European wet grasslands with N, P and K

source_spans.text: Fertilization of European wet grasslands with N, P and K effectively increased aboveground biomass production
Fertilization of European wet grasslands with N, P and K effectively increased aboveground biomass production
The presence of a Carex species-dominated vegetation community at some reclaimed sites shows promise for future reclamation success, if revegetation targets the establishment of this community.
The presence of a Carex species-dominated vegetation community at some reclaimed sites shows promise for future reclamation success, if revegetation targets the establishment of this community.

nodes.name: Strong initial efforts to establish specific vegetation communities

source_spans.text: strong initial efforts to establish specific vegetation communities in reclaimed wetlands may be the best method to increase success
strong initial efforts to establish specific vegetation communities in reclaimed wetlands may be the best method to increase success
The natsedge community produced significantly higher August peak aboveground biomass than either the recsedge or distsal communities
The natsedge community produced significantly higher August peak aboveground biomass than either the recsedge or distsal communities
The distsal community had significantly lower vegetation cover than the natsedge community
The distsal community had significantly lower vegetation cover than the natsedge community
